Welcome to module number three. Excited to have you guys back. I hope you enjoyed module number two. It had a chance to start thinking through the funnels and the value ladders, all those cool exciting things. Now this module we are moving back, we're staying inside the dot-com secrets book. We're gonna be shifting over to section number two, your communication funnels. Now the communication and your follow-up funnels, these are all of the things that a lot of businesses don't spend any time on at all. It drives me nuts. It is the key. We did a test a while ago and we found that for every dollar that we made inside of our front end funnels, we were making over $16 through our follow-up funnels, through our communication funnels, through all the stuff that we are communicating with our audience. And so there's different reasons why we do these communication funnels. Part of it is so that you can, obviously you can sell to your audience. If you understand how to tell stories the right way, and we're gonna get deeper in other modules about storytelling and story selling and things like that, but stories are the most powerful way to sell. But stories also engage, they help build rapport, they help build your culture of people. They are the key. And so you'll notice some overlap with this. In the first module we talked about how to build a culture, right? And how to build a mask. We talked about the charismatic leader or the attractive character and we talked about the other pieces, but we're gonna go deeper into the attractive character and the charismatic leader. How do you create that? How do you position it? How do you how do you figure all those pieces? Like what's your backstory? Why do people care? Like what are your character flaws? There's a whole bunch of important things that that you need to start developing if you're gonna become who you need to be come to lead your people. And that's a pretty important piece of it. And a lot of people get nervous about this and they're like, well I'm not an attractive character. I don't like communicating, I don't like talking. And to be completely honest, I don't either. Like I'm, you ask my wife in the real world, I'm a very shy person. But I'm communicating with the audience. I have to step into my persona and speak to them because people are silently begging to be led and you've got to be that person to do it. So the first part of communications funnels is all about figuring out who is your attractive character. How do you structure that? How do you make it so that people are interested in you? Because people when it all said and done, especially if you look at most businesses, there's others competitors, right? And why someone can pick you over one of your competitors? Sometimes it's based on price, but I don't ever want to be a person fighting over pricing. That's like a horrible way to do business. I want to instead, I want to get people to work, want to work with me because of me. Like if I can do that, then price becomes irrelevant. Like people will pay because they're, they're attracted to you and to your message and who you are. And that is the key is figuring out that piece. So secret number six is all about the attractive character. And then from there, we talk about like when somebody comes into your, and they join your list, they opt in, they become traffic that you own and you should know about that now from the last module. What do you send out to these people? Now, inside of ClickFunnels, we call these follow-up funnels and the tool we use is called actionetics. But the process, the strategy behind it is initially, someone comes in, we take them through what we call a soap opera sequence. These are a bunch of emails to help build the relationship with them. Okay? If you build that relationship initially when they first come into your world, they're not going to leave you. If you don't build that relationship, you just go straight immediately into cell mode. You're not going to have that relationship you need. And so, someone comes in and, and secret number seven goes into, into the soap opera sequences that introduce your audience to you as the attractive character. And then from there, when the soap opera sequence is done, now what do we do? We shift to the next secret, which we're talking about daily Seinfeld emails and how we communicate and we tell stories and we engage and we build rapport and how we do that in a really cool, fun way that gets people to open your messages and read things that you're sending, but also to get them inspired and to want to buy from you. And so, this section of the communication funnels, this is really the glue that glues all the front end and the middle of the value ladder and the back end of the value ladder, all the funnels together is the communication funnels. And so, it's important to spend time here. Most people kind of look at that and they gloss over it, but we wanted you to go deep and spend entire module thinking through this. Okay, when Brandon and Katelyn did, this was the big part, is that they're talking about attractive character. They pause, okay, what's our backstory? What are we sharing with people? And then they, they figured that spend a day figuring that out and say, okay, what's, what are the parables we can tell? And they pause and they try to figure out as many different parables and stories they can tell. And they keep going piece by piece and I want you to do the same thing as well. Spend time on this. It's important. It's the glue that glues all this stuff together. Like I said, these, these communication, these follow-up funnels is what for us, again, last month, just looking at numbers the last month alone, for every dollar made in our front end funnels, made over 16 through these follow-up funnels. And it's all about the communication. That's what glues all these things together. So, master this, become good at it. It's, it's vitally important to your success. And with that said, jump into this module and have some fun. you
